perturbation.original_text: 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided that the term "Configuration" shall not include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided that the term "Setup" shall not include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Configuration' with 'Setup' within the definition of Configuration creates an in-text contradiction because the definition refers to 'Configuration' while attempting to define it using 'Setup', since later on the text only refers to 'Configuration'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings: (1) "Configuration" means the preparation of a computer and related hardware and integration of components into a computer system; provided that the term "Setup" shall not include installation of a computer or related hardware at a customer site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Configuration' with 'Setup' within the definition of 'Configuration'. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, also uses 'Setup' incorrectly. This creates an in-text contradiction because the definition attempts to define 'Configuration' but refers to 'Setup' instead, when the rest of the document only refers to 'Configuration'. This misaligned terminology leads to an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definitions, making the term 'Configuration' unclear and potentially unenforceable, thus causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Client customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Service customers' to 'Client customers' creates potential confusion because the term 'Service Accounts' is defined, but 'Client customers' is not, so, when the text mentions 'existing Service customers', it creates contradiction with the new term, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about whether 'Client customers' and 'Service customers' are intended to be synonymous or distinct.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE III AGREEMENT NOT TO COMPETE 3.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Client customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Service customers' to 'Client customers' introduces a term that is not defined in the contract, while 'Service Accounts' is. This misalignment creates ambiguity and uncertainty about whether 'Client customers' and 'Service customers' are intended to be synonymous or distinct. The contradicted text, which includes both terms, now suffers from in-text contradiction because the term 'Client customers' lacks a clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations based on the Misaligned Terminology definition, since the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Goods for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Product' to 'Goods' in condition (i) while keeping 'Product' in other conditions introduces an in-text contradiction. 'Product' has a defined meaning, whereas 'Goods' does not, creating ambiguity and uncertainty about whether 'Goods' and 'Product' are intended to be synonymous or distinct.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_location: ARTICLE IV Acceptance of Product Orders
perturbation.contradicted_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Goods for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Product' to 'Goods' in condition (i) of the changed text, while keeping 'Product' in conditions (ii), (iii), and (iv), creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity. The original text consistently uses 'Product,' which might have a defined meaning within the contract. Replacing it with 'Goods,' which is undefined, in one instance creates uncertainty about whether the terms are interchangeable or distinct. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, showcasing the ambiguity introduced by the change. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.
